item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP-5025 is deleted so the link doesn't work. Is there gonna be a replacement or something?

item.title: Re: Author's Notes
item.content:encoded: I thought it was just anartists did something wrong and made what they made (since in the world with no canon there are so many instances of anomalous art going wrong, doing harm to people or even anartists themselves).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The article barely tells us anything about KeeLee and I still want to punt them in the face. That's impressive.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was so interesting! A lot of it hit really close to home in ways I didn't think it would. Loved it! :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I hate it too, you’re not alone friend

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
thanks, i hate it.

angry +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is the premise here the Foundation’s documentation has been transformed? Because otherwise this is by no stretch of the imagination an SCP file. As soon as this is linked to by an SCP file about the site I’ll upvote. This isn’t where this goes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Your CSS seems to be broken — the scanline is thick and opaque and obscures whatever you might be reading every few seconds.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was amazingly horrifying in a viscerally unsettling way that makes me feel like my whole body is cringing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is the arritcle want to break the traditional and pedantic concept of gender?
Very good.up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The site would consider them men, thus not allowing them entry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What's 'Hereditary'?

JGT's turned into a GoI now, so it'll have multiple different canons with multiple different ends. In one canon, I'm sure that will happen

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The whole JGT thing reminds me of Hereditary, in that it is a long depiction of abuse from some outside force that always wins no matter how hard it's fought against.

What I'm saying is I desperately need a tale of similar caliber to JGT canon in which KeeLee gets wiped off the face of the earth.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
that part with Landon made me sick to my stomach. +1.

on the subject, though, i wonder what would happen if a trans woman found this blog. would they be able to? or would the site still consider them "men?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is no one else amazed that it says your account name?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Usually I dislike format screws, but something about this one really worked for me. It's equally nostalgic and creepy- the style reminded me of the magazines I used to read as a little girl. I loved the quiz, and the narrator's callous attitude toward the other affected people (particularly Landon) gave me a heavy feeling of dread. I have so many questions. I want to know more about all the characters mentioned, how this anomaly works, and how this situation came to be. I also really liked the linked tale and article— KeeLee is terrifying, especially in those text messages.

All in all, I really like this. Upvoted. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: pink monstrosity is what it is for a reason my friend

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
christ almighty
what is this beautiful abomination

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I guess I'm a girl now? At least I am a cute one?

item.title: Re: ,,♡Von Mädchen, Für Mädchen ♡''
item.content:encoded:
Just Manly Things is a J article I would like to work on in the future, but I’d like for it to be a collab. I do want to get to it eventually (●´ω｀●)

LONG LIVE PINK MONSTROSITY

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I spy with my little eye a crosslink. Thanks for that btw.

item.title: ,,♡Von Mädchen, Für Mädchen ♡''
item.content:encoded:
This SCP can now be read in the German-speaking branch.
Long live pink monstrosity!!! \(◕>◕)/ ~{Yeah!}

Now, DianaBerry, where is "True Boy Stuff"?

item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, I see what you mean. A majority of the downvoted on this come from people disliking format screws.

I can't say that I like 100% of what it does here

I don’t quite get what you mean.

I’m glad you like it! This thing is my baby and took forever to perfect so it being a favorite is nice to hear!

I haven't read all the others comments but oh well.

I am not EXACTLY a fan of format screws, mostly because I think that people abuse them and end up writing things in a strange format when it's not necessary at all, negatively impacting the rest (at least, for me). But this is a really cool example of what I like in a format screw.

I can't say that I like 100% of what it does here, but that's not relevant and my +1 is sure it's one of my favourite Series 5 for now (I am reading aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaall of them only now lol).

The only real critic I must leave is that MAYBE it could have used some more traditional start, or some comment here and there from a Foundation agent.
Yet, as said, I still really like it, this is just something I always say to format screws. I mean, it's cool to see a good screw, but not for this reason the entire standard structure needs to be throw out of the window.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I figured as much. The horror really began to set in when I read about Landon, I visibly paused.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, they were meant to be a trans man.

Also thank you! Pink is best

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Brainwashing in pink, I like it. Also, was the implication with "Landon" was that they were a trans man? Just curious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Everyone does lol! Some people who didn't understand the code thought I was targeting them specifically, and that's pretty funny.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was really invested in the quiz y'know, also when I read my username I got a little heart attack

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah it’s in rewrite but you commented on this one like it was that story when that story is in fact not the same thing as this. Both have JGT, but this is a different page.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think it's in rewrite, actually

CSS still hurts though

item.content:encoded:
This is painful to read, In both ways. My eyes hurt, and I hate "Lolly".

Downvoted to hell.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah I know lol. It’s supposed to look a bit tacky but I am getting help fixing it a bit I think

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: it’s un-ironically my fav too, but too much pink hurts anyones eyes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: but what if pink is unironically my favorite color

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good article, I love it. But css is awful a bit. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I would upvote this, but the theme, like henzoid said, hurts to read. I know it's intentionally bad, but the theme is one of the parts of a page I don't want to intentionally look bad, because it physically hurts me. If you make the theme slightly less bright, i'd upvote, but right now it's a novote.

Edit: I can now comfortably upvote this, sufficiently creepy. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I’m hoping to explore Jess more in a fixture tale. For this skip, it’s supposed to be vague.

And ack. Might go through and try to fix that, probably need to.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah I definitely understand that. I really did want to keep getting crit on it but I think people got tired of me asking since they saw it so much lol. And thank you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I struggle to enjoy the narrative about this anomaly because of my distaste for the theme (that of a vapid, pre-teen pop magazine). To be clear, I don't mean the CSS Theme, and in fact, while I felt unable to read this SCP in the past because of the theme, I now find the colours to be tolerable but would prefer they not be used again. I disgress; I mostly found the subject matter — as it relates to that theme — uninteresting, and I don't even think that's your fault. If I'm not a fan of the concept, then it's just not for me.

Granted, one can represent a disconcerting or distasteful concept in a way that compels the reader, but I don't get that here.

Further thoughts
– hide block

The parts of this that are a traditional Foundation document, are hardly reminiscent of one; there is no solid description of the anomaly, clinical tone is not present, the pacing is jumpy, and the way the Foundation personnel act frankly seems unprofessional and out of character for no real reason.1

I had this next criticism about an earlier iteration of the article, and it unfortunately still stands. How on Earth can such a powerful anomaly exist in the Foundation universe, but dedicate its time to turning girls into "perfect girls"? Yet they can seemingly get the Foundation on its knees just by getting the website on their servers? I suppose that's where the horror comes in, but to me, I don't see the Watsonian reasoning behind it.

Footnotes
1. A researcher accesses the site on a Foundation computer, becomes a powerful reality bender, and uses that power to be more girly. Then, she attacks her fellow researcher, breaches the facility, and is somehow contained. But where, and how? I don't know; it just feels random and inconsistent.

EDIT: Hit save too early by mistake.
All this aside, it's clear I'm in a very small minority here as the article recently hit +100, so I really should commend you for this. And honestly, that's a great achievement. Congrats!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Honestly it's not THAT hard to read, unless someone put an anti-blinding-pink filter on my monitor while I wasn't looking…

It is definitely a monstrosity though, and extremely good. The quiz is a tour de force of the power you can wield when juxtaposing form and content.

I'm not sure I totally get Jess's situation by the end, but I'm OK with that. +1

Minor note: there are some incorrect/inconsistent capitalization issues around "Site/site" (especially pertaining to the blog "site" vs. the containment Site) and at least one non-capitalized "Foundation". CTRL+F should track them down quickly enough.

item.title: Re: -1
item.content:encoded: To me, it has a satisfying justification, and that is that it’s supposed to hurt and be hard to read or even want to look at. But ‘satisfying’ is subjective, so I a hundred percent understand.

Look, I love format screws, I think like 60% of my skips ARE format screws, but a format screw shouldn't literally hurt to read, and although I see a lot of people making jokes about it in the discussion, I just can't in good conscience upvote something that screws with the format this much, this severely, and doesn't have a satisfying justification.

item.title: Re: help
item.content:encoded: I wonder what an araorun would become after it turned into a "perfect girl".

item.title: Re: help
item.content:encoded: The idea is that it is a website that turns people into the "perfect girls", ie, pink monsters

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I think most people would agree with you. It’s just not pink enough.

item.content:encoded: I don't get it and also my eyes are bleeding

item.content:encoded: it's not bad but it needs more pink

I love this so much
